headline: Week of Mar 16-22: Claude's dominance, GPT updates, & Google's scientific AI push shaking up the industry fr

stories.title: Anthropic Drops Claude Channels - Discord & Telegram Integration for Code
stories.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code Channels on Mar 20, letting developers message Claude via Discord/Telegram for persistent, asynchronous coding work. Honestly a game-changer for remote dev workflows.
stories.takeaway: Lowkey killed OpenClaw overnight. No more need for dedicated hardware—just your phone & Claude running in the background.

stories.title: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.4 Mini & Interactive Learning in ChatGPT
stories.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini on Mar 16 with cheaper reasoning fallback for paid users, plus 70+ interactive math/science modules. Speed & affordability flexing hard.
stories.takeaway: Reasoning is now accessible to free users via mini model. Plus interactive formulas let students actually experiment instead of just reading theory.

stories.title: Google DeepMind Launches AI Co-Scientist & AlphaEvolve for National Labs
stories.summary: DeepMind expanded its accelerated access program on Mar 16 with AI co-scientist for biomedical research & AlphaEvolve for algorithm design. Already proving drug candidates & predicting resistance mechanisms.
stories.takeaway: AI is literally compressing years of research into weeks. This isn't hype—it's actually working in real labs right now.

stories.title: Meta Prepping Mango & Avocado Models for H1 2026 Launch
stories.summary: Meta's internally developing Mango (multimodal image/video) & Avocado (better coding LLM) targeting first-half 2026 release via Meta Superintelligence Labs. Trying to catch up fr.
stories.takeaway: Meta knows it's behind & is betting big on world models & coding. If these land, could actually be competitive again. But that's still a big if.

watchNext.title: Codex Security: OpenAI's Cybersecurity Agent Now in Research Preview
watchNext.why: Scored 55.6% on SWE-Bench Pro (software engineering benchmarks). This is the first model that can actually find real vulnerabilities across entire codebases.

watchNext.title: Project Genie Now Live: Google's World Model You Can Actually Use
watchNext.why: Rolling out to AI Ultra subscribers—generate & explore interactive 3D worlds with text prompts. This is the "world model" everyone's been theorizing about, now in production.
